subject: Social Science (History)
overall_learning_objectives: Understand the condition of women in the nineteenth century
Identify major social issues such as child marriage and restrictions on widows
Recognize the role of social reformers in improving society
Understand the importance of women's education
Explain caste discrimination and its effects on society
Understand how print culture helped spread reform ideas
Recognize the long-term impact of social reform movements in India

lesson_plan.title: Introduction to Social Conditions in the 19th Century
topics_covered: Condition of women in the nineteenth century
teaching_strategy: Class discussion about equality and fairness
student_activities: Discuss examples of fairness and inequality
List problems faced by women in the past

lesson_plan.title: Problems Faced by Women
student_activities: Discuss why these practices were harmful

lesson_plan.title: Role of Social Reformers
student_activities: Prepare short notes about one reformer

lesson_plan.title: Women’s Education
student_activities: Create a list of benefits of education
Design a small slogan supporting girls’ education

lesson_plan.title: Caste System and Discrimination
student_activities: Discuss examples of equality and discrimination
Write a short paragraph about social equality

lesson_plan.title: Print Culture and Spread of Reform Ideas
student_activities: Read a short article about social equality

lesson_plan.title: Impact of Social Reforms and Revision
topics_covered: Changes in society due to reforms

teacher_tip: Use storytelling and examples of real historical reformers to make the lesson engaging. Encourage discussions about equality and fairness so that students can connect historical reforms with present-day society.
